engine/rpc/server.go: Remove const searchCandidateScore and its doc comment (dead after this change).
Rewrite the loop building []*hivemindv1.CandidateTopic (previously constant score) to call a new exported-within-package ranking function from search_candidates.go.
Reorder max_results capping to happen AFTER ranking instead of immediately after PrefixScan (see architecture-discovery.md design decision).
Preserve verbatim: nil btreeStore -> empty response; negative max_results -> codes.InvalidArgument; max_results == 0 -> no cap; PrefixScan error -> codes.Internal wrap.

downstream_consumers_checked.usage: query="" (empty prefix) + max_results=pool_size, re-ranks locally via BM25, ignores/ does not depend on SearchCandidates' own score field for correctness
downstream_consumers_checked.risk: none -- empty-query ranking is a no-op (all scores tie), so cap-after-rank degenerates to prior cap-of-raw-PrefixScan-order behavior; pool contents and count unchanged

downstream_consumers_checked.usage: 2-entry fixture (topics/alpha/intro, topics/beta/intro), asserts count and, for the single-match case, path/fileID equality only -- no order assertion across >1 result
downstream_consumers_checked.risk: none -- re-derived expected outputs against new ranking logic in validation-matrix.json, all three subtests remain green

downstream_consumers_checked.usage: exercises SearchCandidates against real on-disk state, asserts against direct store cross-check
downstream_consumers_checked.risk: need to confirm it does not assert an exact CandidateTopic.score value or a specific multi-result order
downstream_consumers_checked.action: grep-checked during implementation before relying on it staying green

downstream_consumers_checked.usage: message shapes only, no changes needed (score field already exists)

not_in_scope: GitHub issue/milestone state (no changes, no push)
regression_surface: go test ./engine/... -v (full module regression) plus go vet ./engine/... and gofmt -l
